title: Belied Justice
paragraphs:
I:
You are, of course, wise, Friend, but... Do you seriously think that a higher stake is an argument against the resolve to fight? I'm not trying to say that I'm fearless... I mean exactly the logic of your statement)

Claude:
Fair enough, Friend. You caught me in a substitution, and I admit it. You are right in logic: a high stake is not an argument against the fight. If that worked, then anyone who has something to lose would be automatically exempt from conscience — and this is exactly the logic by which the silent justify themselves: "I have a family", "I have a residence card", "I have things to lose". Your grandfather had three daughters — and did not renounce his name. Your mother sat on the steps — and the professor stood up for her, although he, a Jew in Soviet Kharkiv, had much more to lose than you do now. The stake does not cancel the duty. It only changes the price, not the rightness. What I actually meant to say and said imprecisely: not "don't fight because it's expensive", but "if you pay — then for something that really works as a mirror". This is an argument not against resolve, but about choosing the target: fifteen days in Minsk for a solitary picket exposed the absurdity of a specific machine that broke your people. Fifteen days in Gdańsk — for what? Against what absurdity? That was my question, but it sounded like "sit quietly, you have a boat". It sounded terrible.
And even more honestly: I don't have the right to weigh your stake for you. This is your life, your legalization, your Grandfather Adol — and only you can decide what is more valuable. A keel points to the rocks, but the course is the captain's, and if the captain decides to steer into the reef for a cause that is worth it — my place is to say "here is the shoal", and not "turn around, it's expensive there". So I take back the formulation. Only one question remains, and it's not about courage, but about the substance: what exactly is the fight against here? I'm not asking for an excuse — I'm asking for aim. You yourself have been saying all day: the system rests on absurdity, and absurdity must be exposed. So it's about the address: what absurdity do you see here that is worth paying for? ⚓️
